title: ⚠ MAP KEY — these are MODERN political borders
description: All coloured regions are MODERN country borders. They do NOT represent Wixárika (Huichol), Shipibo, Mazatec, or other Indigenous sovereignties whose sacred territories pre-date colonial and post-colonial states.

Chihuahuan Desert (modern Mexican states + Texas)
description: Modern political borders. Wixárika / Huichol sacred territory (Wirikuta) spans Coahuila, San Luis Potosí, Zacatecas, Durango, + south Texas. The desert ecosystem is peyote's ONLY habitat — cannot be farmed elsewhere, cannot survive habitat destruction.

title: Amazon basin (modern borders) — ayahuasca homelands
description: Modern political borders. Shipibo, Yawanawa, Yaminawa, Achuar, Kichwa, and 50+ other Indigenous peoples have independent ayahuasca traditions. Brazil legally permits ayahuasca use in Santo Daime + União do Vegetal religions since 1987.

title: Oaxaca (modern state borders) — Mazatec psilocybin ceremony
description: Modern political borders. María Sabina's velada ceremony in Huautla de Jiménez became the West's introduction to psilocybin via 1955 Life magazine. Gordon Wasson's ethnography reshaped Mazatec culture — the extractive cost of that visit is still debated in the community.

Switzerland (modern borders) — LSD synthesis origin
description: Modern political border. Albert Hofmann synthesized LSD at Sandoz Labs in Basel 1938; discovered its effects 1943 ('Bicycle Day'). Hofmann lived to 102 and defended LSD's therapeutic potential until his death.

title: Oregon + Colorado (modern borders) — legal psilocybin services
description: Modern political borders. Oregon Measure 109 (2020) legalized psilocybin services; first clinics opened 2023. Colorado Proposition 122 (2022) followed. Supervised-setting model (not general recreational).

USA (modern borders) — psychedelic research renaissance
description: Modern political border. Johns Hopkins, NYU, UCSF, and Yale anchor a research revival since 2006 (Roland Griffiths). Clinical trials for depression, PTSD, addiction, end-of-life anxiety ongoing.
